epoch.title: Attack & Defense scoreboard
epoch.compact_description: Settled is the primary rank value; exact ties use Live, offense, defense, SLA, then participation ID.
epoch.not_started: Scoring is waiting for a complete roster and prepared exact checkers.
epoch.load_error: The A&D scoreboard could not be loaded.
epoch.mobile_hint: Select a team to see every challenge score.
epoch.footer_hint: Defense is positive. Cell tint shows latest health. Updated {{time}}.

score_info.title: How scoring works
score_info.tick_body: A tick and a round are the same live cycle. Fresh flags are planted, checkers run, and captures plus SLA evidence are recorded.
score_info.epoch_current_body: Current Epoch {{epoch}} covers scoring rounds {{start}}-{{end}}. It appears in Live while open and joins Settled only after its checks finish and flag submission windows close.
score_info.epoch_body: An epoch groups {{count}} scoring rounds. It appears in Live while open and joins Settled only after its checks finish and flag submission windows close.
score_info.epoch_weight: Old flags may remain valid in later rounds; a capture counts in the epoch where its flag was planted. Complete epochs have equal weight, while a shortened final epoch is weighted by rounds completed.
score_info.intro: Challenge contributions add up to the team total and already include challenge and epoch weighting.
score_info.offense: Offense measures accepted capture coverage, with a small bounded rarity bonus for uncommon captures.
score_info.defense: Defense measures the share of eligible opponent-flag pairs that remain uncaptured.
score_info.sla: SLA is checker-measured reliability and multiplies the whole local score.
score_info.formula: Local score = 100 × SLA × (40% offense + 40% defense + 20% √(offense × defense))
score_info.settlement: Rank is ordered by Settled score. Exact ties use Live, offense, defense, SLA, then participation ID. Live includes the open epoch and can change until it settles.

detail.current_warning: Epoch {{epoch}} is still live. Orange values are provisional; the team Live total is the first tie-break when Settled scores are exactly equal.
detail.recent_only: Latest {{limit}} epochs; totals include every epoch.
detail.settled_hint: Finalized challenge contribution. Challenge contributions add up to the team total; orange text is the live projection.

evidence.sparse: No eligible flag evidence is available yet. Live values remain provisional.
evidence.no_defense: No defense opportunities are qualified yet; exact healthy checks are required.

ad.next_tick_tip: Time until the next tick — services are re-checked and flags rotate every {{seconds}}s.

flags_syncing.label: Flags syncing — wait before attacking
flags_syncing.description: This round’s flags are still syncing. Wait before attacking or updating your defended flag.

flag_delivery_failed.label: {{count}} flag deliveries need attention
flag_delivery_failed.description: {{count}} service did not acknowledge this round’s flag. The operator has been notified; health evidence will identify affected services.

no_service.title: No service for your team yet
no_service.description: If you expected a container here, it hasn't been provisioned yet. Ask the operator to run "Ensure containers" from the A&D Ops console.

ad.token_captain_only: Only your team captain can generate the API token. Ask them to open this panel and click Generate token.
ad.api_note: Batch submission only — pass one or more captured flags in a single request (max 100). Per-flag results are returned in input order.

token_modal.title: Your new A&D API token
token_modal.warning: Save this token now — it will not be shown again. The previous token (if any) has been invalidated.

empty.title: No A&D challenges in this game
empty.description: If you expected to see services here, the operator hasn't created any A&D challenges or your team's containers haven't been provisioned yet.

defend.title: Defend
defend.description: Your team's services. Keep them healthy + patched. Click any IP or flag to copy.

attack.title: Attack
attack.description: Submit a flag you captured from another team.

guide.title: Attack & Defense — Toolkit
guide.intro: Everything you need to play A&D: your personal API token, your VPN config, the API contract, and the rules. The first two sections are actionable — token rotation and VPN config download.

token.title: Your API token
token.intro: A personal Bearer token scoped to you + this game. Your exploit scripts pass it as Authorization: Bearer <token> when submitting captured flags. Every team member manages their own — rotating yours does not affect anyone else, and if you get kicked from the team your token stops working immediately.

vpn.title: VPN config
vpn.intro: Per-user WireGuard config. The first download generates a fresh keypair + assigns you an IP from the game subnet; subsequent downloads return the same file. Drop the .conf into wg-quick (or wireguard-tools / the WireGuard app) to join the A&D network.
vpn.operator_note: If the downloaded file contains <unconfigured-server:...> placeholders, your operator has not finished provisioning the WireGuard server. The keypair + assigned IP in your .conf are still valid; the operator just needs to set the server endpoint + server pubkey for the connection to come up.
vpn.linux_hint: Linux: sudo wg-quick up ./ad-game-….conf. macOS / Windows: import via the official WireGuard app.

rounds.title: Rounds & flags
rounds.tick: A round (tick) is the scoring unit. Length is set per game — every A&D service shares one tick (the operator picks it, typically 60–180s).
rounds.plant: At each tick the platform writes a fresh flag into your container (the "current flag"). You can see it in the challenge modal — protect it.
rounds.lifetime: Old flags stay valid for N ticks (default 5). After that they expire and cannot be accepted as scoring evidence.
rounds.warmup: Round 0 is warmup — no scoring yet, just make sure your service is up.

scoring.title: How scoring works
scoring.intro: The official scoreboard settles accepted outcomes per service inside equal-budget epochs:
scoring.attack: C is your accepted-capture coverage over the frozen opponent roster. H adds a bounded capturer-count rarity fraction. An accepted submission records evidence immediately, but it has no standalone point value; the epoch settles all outcomes together.
scoring.defense: Every exact healthy custom-check flag creates one pair per frozen opponent. Each distinct capturer removes only its own protected pair. An unstolen pair is observable non-capture, not proof that an exploit was attempted.
scoring.sla: A clean passing check earns 1.0, recovery earns 0.5, and a failed or missing check earns 0. InternalError carries the last scored non-infrastructure result after startRound. An isolated first error earns 0; only an all-service first-error outage voids the challenge-round sample for the full roster.
scoring.total_formula: Service = 100 × R × (0.40A + 0.40D + 0.20√(A×D)); normalized service weights form the epoch score, and finalized epochs determine official rank.

targets.title: Find your targets
targets.intro: A&D is attack-first — you need to know every other team's container IP per service. This endpoint is the canonical list. Excludes your own team and waits until the warmup round has elapsed (currentRound > 0).
targets.poll_note: Poll once per round (~tickSeconds). Container IPs are stable across rounds unless a team Resets or an admin Stops the container — then a fresh IP shows up here within ~15s.
targets.tip: lastCheckStatus tells you which targets are healthy: Ok / Mumble / Offline. Prioritize Ok ones — Offline containers won't accept your exploit, and Mumble usually means the service is broken in a way that won't expose the flag.

submit.title: How to submit captured flags
submit.api_only: Flag submission is API-only — there is no web form for A&D. Your exploit scripts batch the flags they collected and POST them in one request.
submit.token_steps: Open any A&D challenge in the challenges grid. If you are the team captain, click "Generate token" / "Rotate token" — the plaintext appears exactly once. Save it somewhere your exploit scripts can read.
submit.batch_note: Pass 1 to 100 flag strings per request. Results return in input order so you can correlate.
submit.statuses: Per-flag status is one of: accepted | duplicate | wrong | expired | self_attack | not_started. Accepted flags become epoch evidence; the submit response does not assign immediate points.

container.title: Your container
container.ip: The challenge modal shows your container IP:port — that's where checks run and where attackers point their exploits.
container.flag_file: The live flag is rewritten every round at the path in the RSCTF_FLAG_FILE environment variable. Read it fresh from $RSCTF_FLAG_FILE on every request (don't cache it).
container.flag_env: Never hard-code a platform-specific path. A creation-time RSCTF_FLAG compatibility value may exist, but it becomes stale after rotation — always read $RSCTF_FLAG_FILE.
container.patch: Patch your service live inside the container — the platform does not redeploy on its own. Your changes live ONLY in the running container; there is no persistent disk.
container.supervisor: Your service runs under a supervisor (PID 1). If the service process crashes — or you restart it — it comes back automatically and your patches stay. Restarting the service is safe; but don't kill PID 1 (the supervisor): that drops the box back to the base image.
container.reset: Reset rebuilds the box from the original image — it WIPES all your changes (patches included), costs SLA during the rebuild, and has a cooldown. Use it only if your box is wedged or compromised, then re-apply your patches.

do_dont.title: Do / Don't
do_dont.do_patch: Patch your service before the first attack lands.
do_dont.do_automate: Automate flag submission — paste into a loop that fetches the current flag from each target and POSTs the batch.
do_dont.do_monitor: Watch your check status badge — if it goes Mumble/Offline you are losing SLA every tick.
do_dont.do_reapply: Re-apply your patches after any reset — a reset reverts to the pristine, vulnerable image.
do_dont.dont_share: Share your flags or your token with other teams — operators detect it and apply penalties.
do_dont.dont_dos: DoS another team's service or the checker infrastructure — instant DQ.
do_dont.dont_break: Patch in a way that breaks the legit check (returns 500 to the checker) — you lose SLA equivalent to being offline.
do_dont.dont_hardcode: Hard-code the path or cache the flag — read $RSCTF_FLAG_FILE fresh each request, or you'll serve a stale flag and fail the check.
do_dont.dont_kill_pid1: Kill PID 1 (the supervisor) in your box — it resets you to the base image and wipes your patches.

content.game_lasts_long: The game will last for a long time

content: Are you sure you want to register for this game?

After successfully registering for the game and passing the review, the team will be locked and you will no longer be able to remove any member from the team. The team will be unlocked after the game ends or when the participation request is rejected.
The limit on the number of players refers to the number of members that have decided to participate as part of the specific team, regardless of which team you choose to play with.

ad.reset: Rebuild this container to baseline (you lose SLA during the rebuild)
ad.snapshot: Download your container as a loadable Docker image (docker load -i …)

submit.title: Submit a challenge
submit.subtitle: Share a challenge with the game organizers for review.
submit.review_notice: Submissions are hidden from participants until an admin approves.
submit.disabled_notice: User submissions are turned off for this game by the organizer.
submit.tarball_help: Upload a .tar.gz of one challenge directory (challenge.yaml + attachments).
submit.archive_help: Upload a .tar.gz or .zip of one challenge directory (challenge.yaml + attachments).

dropzone.title: Drag your archive here, or click to pick one
dropzone.hint: Accepts .tar.gz or .zip up to 64 MB. Must contain challenge.yaml at the root.
dropzone.rejected: File type or size not accepted.

example.title: Challenge templates
example.intro: Pick the type that matches your challenge and download the template. Edit the files, zip the folder, then upload below.
example.static_attachment_desc: Files-only challenge. Players download whatever you put in dist/; flag is matched server-side from the flags: list. Use for crypto, reverse, misc, anything that doesn't need a live server.
example.dynamic_container_desc: A separate container per team, each with a unique flag injected via the RSCTF_FLAG environment variable. Prevents flag-sharing.
example.attack_defense_desc: Persistent per-team service plus a checker. The platform plants a fresh flag into /flag every tick and rotates it — no flags: or flagTemplate: block. Defenders patch the bug; attackers exploit other teams' instances.

tips.value_ignored: value: in the YAML is ignored — the admin sets the score after review.
tips.visible_ignored: visible: in the YAML is ignored — admin chooses when to enable.
tips.review_queue: Submissions sit in the admin review queue; they are NOT live until approved.
tips.template_image: For container types, omit containerImage entirely — the platform auto-builds ./src/Dockerfile. Only set it if you want to override (e.g. "ghcr.io/..." for an already-published image).
tips.dynamic_flag_env: For Dynamic Container, the platform sets the RSCTF_FLAG environment variable inside each team's container. Reference it from src/run.sh (the template's run.sh shows the pattern); never hard-code a flag.
tips.solver_folder: Put a working solver under solver/ so admins (and your future self) can verify the challenge still works after edits.
tips.size_cap: Archive must be ≤ 64 MB. Single challenge per archive.
